assertions.description: The bus_topology detected CS, MISO, MOSI, SCK prefixes indicating SPI. I2C signals SCL0/SDA0 are present in nets and design_analysis classifies SCL0 as 'clock' and SDA0 as 'data'. BOM and component data are faithfully extracted.

The USB-C connector HRO-TYPE-C-31-M-12 has no 5.1k CC pulldown resistors, no VBUS decoupling cap, and no explicit ESD IC. All four checks failing is accurate — the design appears to use USB-C only for mechanical/power connection without proper USB-C sink configuration.

D6 (P6SMB6.8CA TVS diode) is correctly identified as a protection device with protected_net='+5V' and clamp_net='GND'. The decoupling analysis correctly notes only one 0.1µF cap on +3V3 and flags U1/U2 as lacking decoupling on +5V and +BATT.

U3 is an SP3485EN (RS-485 transceiver) — identifiable from both its value and LCSC/datasheet. The signal_analysis.protection_devices correctly found D6 as TVS, but no RS-485 bus detection occurred. The design has /A, /B nets (RS-485 differential pair) and /DE (direction enable), /RO (receiver output), /DI (driver input) which are classic RS-485 signals. This is a missed detection.
